items.title: Media zien Salah 'briljante vorm' hervinden en loven 'tactische verrassing' Slot
items.summary: De Engelse media waren vol lof over Liverpool na de ruime FA Cup-zege op Brighton & Hove Albion . Er was veel aandacht voor Mohamed Salah, die zijn topvorm lijkt te hervinden bij de kampioen van Engeland. Maar ook een 'tactische verrassing' van Arne Slot werd aangestipt.

items.title: VI Live: lastig uitduel voor NEC, Napoli tegen AS Roma
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Hoe een dertigjarige Nederlander td werd aan de andere kant van de wereld
items.summary: In Balverliefd aandacht voor de gang van zaken achter de schermen van het voetbal en opvallende gebeurtenissen aan de rand van het veld. Deze week over Mitch van Gellekom, de dertigjarige directeur van Adelaide United.

items.title: Is Zubimendi Arsenal's best player?
items.summary: After an impressive start to his Arsenal career, BBC Sport looks at whether Martin Zubimendi is the Gunners' most important player.

items.title: Three years on, Deulofeu is still fighting for 'miracle' return
items.summary: Gerard Deulofeu has not played for more than three years but believes he can make "history" by fighting for a "miracle" comeback.

items.title: VI Live: Feyenoord krijgt bezoek van in crisis verkerend Go Ahead
items.summary: Feyenoord krijgt zondagmiddag bezoek van Go Ahead Eagles. De Rotterdammers kwamen in rustiger vaarwater na de zege op FC Utrecht. Go Ahead wacht al zeventien duels op een zege. Volg de Eredivisie-pot in deze VI Live (aftrap 12.15 uur).

items.title: Silky Lass takes on Lakota Chief at Newcastle
items.summary: It's all about Newcastle today, with Silky Lass and Lakota Chief headlining the feature race at 4.42pm.

items.title: Edwin van der Sar als directeur bij Manchester United? 'Eerste aan wie ik dacht'
items.summary: Gary Neville zou een terugkeer van Edwin van der Sar als bestuurder bij Manchester United enorm toejuichen. In een reportage op VI PRO laat Neville daar geen misverstand over bestaan.

items.title: Real Madrid 'lucky to have' Trent, says Arbeloa
items.summary: Alvaro Arbeloa praised Trent Alexander-Arnold, saying Real Madrid are "lucky to have" him after the full-back starred, providing an assist, in Real Madrid's 4-1 win over Real Sociedad.

items.title: Juve furious at 'unacceptable' ref call in Inter loss
items.summary: Juventus officials expressed their fury and what they called an "unacceptable" and "embarrassing" decision to send off defender Pierre Kalulu in Saturday's dramatic loss to rivals Inter Milan.

items.title: Salah: In-form Szoboszlai among the world's best
items.summary: Mohamed Salah has described teammate Dominik Szoboszlai as "one of the best players in the world" after the Hungary international scored for Liverpool in their 3-0 victory over Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday.

items.title: Ronaldo ends protest, scores 18 minutes later
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo returned to Al Nassr's lineup for Saturday's match at Al Fateh -- and swiftly scored.

items.title: Not done yet: Salah sparkles in Liverpool's FA Cup win
items.summary: Mo Salah turning in a performance in front of an adoring crowd? No, it's not 2024, but it sure feels like it.

items.title: Salah shines as Liverpool comfortably beat Brighton
items.summary: Goals from Curtis Jones, Dominik Szoboszlai and Mohamed Salah give Liverpool a comfortable 3-0 win over Brighton in the fourth round of the FA Cup at Anfield.

items.title: Mohamed Salah recaptures scintillating form as Liverpool see off Brighton
items.summary: There was rancour and recrimination when Mohamed Salah last faced Brighton, at Anfield in December, along with doubt over whether he would be seen in a Liverpool shirt again. Fast forward two months and the Egyptian great is starting, scoring and shaping games for Arne Slot again. Appeasement between the pair is for the greater good. Salah produced a sublime assist, his fourth since returning from the Africa Cup of Nations, and scored from the penalty spot as Liverpool moved into round five with a commanding victory over Fabian Hürzeler’s struggling team. There was no evidence of Brighton not performing for their under-pressure manager but their lack of cutting edge was glaring, as was the case when visiting here in the Premier League. Continue reading...

items.title: 'The officials looked petrified' - was Villa Park chaos advert for VAR?
items.summary: Newcastle United beat Aston Villa in the FA Cup fourth round in a match dominated by a number of controversial decisions without VAR.

items.title: Aston Villa latest: 'Referees need VAR,' says Emery after Newcastle loss
Keith Andrews’s gravity-defying miracle has Brentford dreaming of Europe | Jonathan Wilson
items.summary: Their best players and managers may move on, but this thoroughly modern club keep punching above their weight When the news cycle spins so fast, it’s worth remembering where Brentford were in the summer. They had lost their popular manager of seven years, Thomas Frank. They had lost their two best forwards, Bryan Mbeumo and Yoane Wissa. They had lost their goalkeeper Mark Flekken. And they had lost two stalwarts in Christian Nørgaard and Ben Mee (even if the latter’s involvement the previous season had been limited as he turned 35). Departure and replacement is an unavoidable part of life for a club such as Brentford, but this seemed a like a lot to deal with. Their summer signings were hard to judge. As a rule of thumb, if Brentford are signing someone about whom you already have considered opinions, it’s likely something has gone awry. That said, Caoimhín Kelleher’s gifts are clear, and a fee of just under £13m seemed good value for a goalkeeper with Premier League experience, while Dango Ouattara had demonstrated at Bournemouth how effective he could be either through the middle or out wide. But Antoni Milambo, Michael Kayode and Kaye Furo were unknown quantities. Continue reading...

items.title: Sandro Tonali fires Newcastle past 10-man Aston Villa on bad night for officials
items.summary: Football’s interminable video assistant refereeing debate has its latest chapter after a bizarre evening and officiating performance at Villa Park. With the FA Cup not allowing the video protocol until the fifth round, this was an occasion to make the abolitionists think twice. Is elite football already too far gone to officiate without a bank of screens in a faraway business park? Or were Chris Kavanagh, a referee promoted to the Uefa elite list in December, and his assistants just having a nightmare day at the office? Sandro Tonali’s two goals and Nick Woltemade’s clincher booked Newcastle’s fifth-round place, completing a comeback in the face of officiating mistakes weighing against Eddie Howe’s team. It was Aston Villa who lost their discipline. They should have been down to 10 men earlier than they eventually were. Continue reading...

items.title: HORROR HOLE! | McIlroy makes triple bogey at Pebble Beach
items.summary: Rory McIlroy drops three shots as he makes triple bogey at the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am.
